key_departures.impact: Traded for a 2026 fifth-round pick. Howard was Houston's best offensive lineman in 2025, starting at RT, LG, and RG. He allowed pressure on just 3.4% of snaps (career best). Second consecutive year the Texans traded away their best OL — Tunsil in 2025, Howard in 2026. Leaves Houston with only two returning starters on the OL.

key_departures.new_team: Detroit Lions (trade, part of Montgomery deal)
key_departures.impact: 2023 second-round pick traded to Detroit as part of the David Montgomery package. Started just one game in 2025 but was a key interior depth piece and center option. Losing him further depletes interior OL depth and removes the in-house center competition.

Released before the start of the league year, clearing $8M in cap space. Mixon missed all of 2025 with a foot injury. His release was expected and does not create a new need as Montgomery was acquired to replace him.
Departed to Atlanta. Was a depth/rotational linebacker; loss creates minor depth thinning at LB but not a starting hole given Al-Shaair, To'oTo'o, and Speed returning.
Lost to free agency. Was a rotational interior defensive lineman. Some depth lost on the DL but Rankins re-signed and Logan Hall added, mitigating impact.
Not expected to return per KPRC2's Aaron Wilson. Was an aging veteran depth piece behind Hunter and Anderson. Mitigated by the Logan Hall and Dominique Robinson signings.
Not expected to return. Rushed for 506 yards on 122 carries in 15 games in 2025 but was still slowed by his 2023 knee injury. Departure covered by the David Montgomery trade.
Not expected to return. Was a rotational receiver behind Nico Collins. Loss creates some WR depth need, but does not eliminate a true starter.
Not re-signed; replaced via trade for Kai Kroeger. Townsend had declining stats in 2025. The Kroeger trade directly replaced this spot so net need impact is neutral.
Depth EDGE rusher not re-signed. Covered by Logan Hall and Dominique Robinson signings.

signings.analysis: One-year, $40.1M fully guaranteed extension keeps Hunter through 2027. He posted 15 sacks in 2025 (all 17 games), earning Second Team All-Pro honors. The deal makes him the 4th-highest-paid edge rusher in the NFL. EDGE was not a need pre-FA and remains rock-solid. The 1-year structure is notable — Houston and Hunter continue their annual extension pattern, meaning EDGE will need to be revisited again next offseason. Will Anderson Jr.'s extension is the next domino.

Three-year, $37.5M re-signing with $23.5M guaranteed locks in the best remaining offensive lineman on the roster through 2028. Ingram was a top-10 PFF run-blocking guard in 2025 and would have commanded $15–18M/year on the open market. At $12.5M AAV, this is well-below projected market and secures the right guard spot long-term. RG is fully addressed. Center (Jake Andrews) and LT (Aireontae Ersery, 2nd year) remain ongoing concerns, but the most critical interior free agent is locked up.

One-year, $12.6M extension through 2027. Schultz led the Texans in targets in 2025 with a career-high 82 catches for 777 yards and 3 TDs. TE was not a need, and this re-signing keeps the position stable through 2027. Foster Moreau added as depth. TE is in good shape.

Two-year deal with full $12M guaranteed. Rankins had a resurgent year in 2025: 3 sacks, 35 tackles, 44 pressures, and two touchdowns. Re-signing him stabilizes the IDL with Rankins alongside incumbent rotation pieces. The $12M total guaranteed signals real commitment for a 31-year-old. IDL need is addressed at the starter level, though age means they'll likely want a young piece in the draft still.

contract.notes: Worth up to $7M; exact guarantees not reported

signings.analysis: One-year deal worth up to $7M. Brown started 7 games in 2025 and allowed the 6th-lowest pressure rate (7.1%) among RT starters Weeks 10–16. Health is the primary concern — knee and ankle issues have limited him. At $7M/year on a 1-year deal, this is a bridge not a solution. The Texans lost Tytus Howard and traded Scruggs, leaving OT a critical need. Brown and 2025 2nd-rounder Aireontae Ersery form the likely starting pair but the depth and long-term outlook remain thin. OT need partially addressed — draft investment still required.

Two-year, $25M deal ($13.5M fully guaranteed) with the former Colts veteran. Smith was a starter for 8 seasons in Indianapolis, playing in 107 games (starting 105). He ranked 21st in pressure rate allowed among tackles with 300+ pass block snaps (7.8%). Concerns include injury history. This is a legitimate starting-caliber signing that stabilizes the right side alongside Brown and Ersery, but the 2-year structure and age (30) mean the long-term OT picture still warrants a draft investment. Combined with the Brown re-sign, OT is partially addressed — no longer a panic need but still one for the draft.

contract.notes: Existing Lions contract: $5.49M in 2026, $7.49M in 2027; no guaranteed money remaining. Trade cost: OL Juice Scruggs + 2026 4th-round pick + 2027 7th-round pick

signings.analysis: Acquired via trade from Detroit (cost: Scruggs + 2026 4th + 2027 7th). Montgomery is expected to be the lead back, paired with sophomore Woody Marks. Houston was 29th in yards-per-attempt in 2025 and dead last in yards after contact. Montgomery brings proven power-running (2,506 yards and 33 TDs over 3 Lions seasons) and short-yardage capability. The loss of the 4th-round pick is meaningful cost but RB was a clear need after releasing Mixon and losing Chubb. RB need fully addressed. Draft capital should now pivot elsewhere.

Three-year, $24.75M deal ($16.5M guaranteed). Blankenship started 50 games over three Eagles seasons and is a Super Bowl champion. His 2025 was a down year (1 INT, down from 4 in 2024) but he was 27 and a team captain in Philly. At $8.25M AAV he's mid-tier starter pricing. He joins Calen Bullock in a two-safety starting set. This fills the open safety spot MJ Stewart vacated in effectiveness (Stewart re-signed on a vet deal). S need fully addressed for 2026 with a committed 3-year investment.

contract.notes: Up to $13M max value; exact guarantees not publicly confirmed

signings.analysis: Two-year deal worth up to $13M. Speed started 9 games in his first Texans season and is the No. 3 LB behind Al-Shaair and To'oTo'o. He fills a hybrid LB/special teams role. The Christian Harris departure opens a depth spot, but Speed's re-signing maintains the core LB group. Azeez Al-Shaair's extension remains the more pressing LB business. LB depth is adequately addressed by Speed's return but the room still needs Al-Shaair locked in.

Two-year, $6M re-sign. Stewart is a versatile DB and special teams contributor. At $3M AAV this is clearly a depth/special teams signing. Blankenship handles the starter need. Stewart provides continuity as a chess piece in the secondary.

One-year deal worth up to $4M. Robinson was a depth piece for Chicago for 4 seasons. ESPN notes he will fill the same depth role for Houston. Provides rotational EDGE depth behind Hunter and Anderson as Autry and Barnett depart. EDGE depth is thin beyond the top two but this keeps the room functional.

Two-year, $6.3M deal. Moreau had 413 receiving yards in 2024 (2nd-most of his career) and adds depth behind Schultz. TE was already addressed via Schultz's extension. This is pure depth/insurance at a team-friendly price.
One-year, $2.5M re-sign. Jones is a veteran IDL depth piece. With Rankins re-signed and Logan Hall (EDGE/IDL tweener) and Settle departing, Jones keeps the rotation intact cheaply. Depth move only.

contract.notes: ESPN reported $14M deal; PFR/Tom Pelissero reported $7M total — discrepancy noted, ESPN figure used as higher credibility source given ESPN tracked the FA signing directly. Guarantees not publicly confirmed.

signings.analysis: Two-year deal (value reported between $7M–$14M total across sources; ESPN pegged at $14M). Hall is a 2022 second-round pick of the Buccaneers who had 5.5 sacks in 2024 but only 1.5 in 2025. At 25, he has upside in Houston's scheme and will compete for the first rotational EDGE spot behind Hunter and Anderson. Replaces Autry and Barnett in the depth chart. This is a bounce-back opportunity deal — not a need-filler at the starter level. EDGE depth improved but need remains for a developmental piece long-term.

contract.notes: Makes him the highest-paid kicker in NFL history at signing

signings.analysis: Two-year, $13M extension. Fairbairn tied the NFL record with 44 field goals in 2025. K was not a need and this simply locks in a franchise kicker at record-setting money. No draft impact.

contract.notes: Existing rookie contract ($1.005M in 2026, $1.112M in 2027). Trade cost: 2028 6th-round pick for Kroeger + 2028 7th-round pick

signings.analysis: Acquired via trade swapping late 2028 picks. Directly replaces Tommy Townsend who departed. Kroeger averaged 44.8 yards on 56 punts in his Saints rookie season. Houston gets a young, cost-controlled punter. No meaningful need impact — one punter replaced another.

contract.notes: Terms not publicly disclosed as of 2026-03-13

signings.analysis: Re-signed as a veteran depth DB and special teams contributor. CB starting spots (Stingley, Lassiter) are already set. This is a depth/special teams retention.

contract.notes: Signed per Texans official 'Just Signed' page; contract terms not publicly reported as of 2026-03-13

signings.analysis: Listed on Texans' official Just Signed page. Safety depth addition after Blankenship and Stewart handle the starter/backup roles. Terms not yet public.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Tytus Howard was traded to Cleveland (5th-round pick return), stripping the Texans of their best OL. The Braden Smith signing (2yr/$25M) and Trent Brown re-sign (1yr/$7M) provide two veteran starters but neither is a long-term anchor. Smith is 30 with injury history; Brown is 32 on a one-year deal. Aireontae Ersery (2nd-year 2025 pick) projects as the LT. Center Jake Andrews is the only other returning starter. OT/OL overall remains a top-2 draft priority — should target a high-upside OT in rounds 1–2 to build around Ersery and provide long-term security. Need stays at tier 2.

Ed Ingram's 3yr/$37.5M re-sign solves RG. However, Juice Scruggs was traded away, and center is now a glaring concern. Jake Andrews projects as the starting C but Houston missed out on free agent centers (Linderbaum to Raiders, Cade Mays to Lions). No veteran C was added in FA. IOL need drops from critical (tier 1) to elevated (tier 2) thanks to the Ingram re-sign, but C remains a draft target in rounds 2–3.

David Montgomery trade fully addresses the lead RB role. He steps in as the starter alongside Woody Marks. Joe Mixon was released, Nick Chubb departed. The backfield is now properly stocked. RB drops from a tier-2 need to a tier-4 luxury — no meaningful draft investment warranted here.

Reed Blankenship's 3yr/$24.75M signing ($16.5M guaranteed) fills the open safety spot alongside Calen Bullock. MJ Stewart re-signed for depth. Safety drops from a meaningful need to fully addressed. No draft investment needed at S in 2026.

Sheldon Rankins re-signed on a 2yr/$12M fully guaranteed deal. Naquan Jones re-signed for depth. Tim Settle Jr. departed but was rotational. Logan Hall can play as a 3-4 end/DT tweener. IDL drops from a tier-2 need to a tier-3 depth consideration. A young IDL piece in rounds 3–5 of the draft would still be smart given Rankins' age (31).

Danielle Hunter's 1yr/$40.1M extension and Will Anderson Jr. (under contract) keep the starting pair elite. Depth was thinned with Autry/Barnett departures but Logan Hall (2yr/$14M) and Dominique Robinson (1yr/$4M) partially refill the room. EDGE drops slightly as a need — still worth a day-2 or day-3 pick for developmental depth given Anderson's extension will be massive and Hunter is on annual deals.

Christian Kirk's departure creates a depth/slot WR void. Nico Collins is the clear WR1. Jaylin Noel and Tank Dell (returning from injury) are the other known options. No WR was added in free agency. WR remains a tier-3 need — not critical given Collins, but the slot depth is thin and a mid-round WR in the draft makes sense.

E.J. Speed re-signed (2yr/$13M) and Christian Harris departed to Atlanta. Al-Shaair (potential extension pending) and Henry To'oTo'o anchor the group. LB depth is functional but Azeez Al-Shaair's extension situation remains unresolved — if he departs, this escalates sharply. Hold at tier 3 pending Al-Shaair resolution. The Texans should prioritize getting that extension done.

Combined note with IOL above — Houston's offensive line remains the most pressing offseason concern heading into the draft even after FA spending. Two starters (Howard, Scruggs) were traded out. While Ingram, Brown, Smith, and Ersery provide a workable group, the line has only one established long-term piece (Ingram). Draft must prioritize OL in the first two days.

key_additions.headline: 15-sack All-Pro re-signed on historic 1-year deal, locking in the elite pass-rush duo with Anderson through 2027
Top-10 PFF run-blocking guard retained on below-market deal, anchoring the rebuilt offensive interior through 2028
Power back acquired to fix Houston's league-worst run game after Mixon release and Chubb departure
8-year Colts starter signed to stabilize the offensive tackle room after Tytus Howard trade
Super Bowl champion safety fills the open starting spot alongside Calen Bullock in Houston's elite secondary
Disruptive DT re-signed on fully guaranteed deal to maintain the league's top-ranked interior defensive line
Career-high 82-catch TE locked in through 2027 as Stroud's top safety valve
Veteran RT re-signed on team-friendly deal to provide starting-caliber continuity after Howard trade
2022 second-round pick joins Houston's elite defensive line as the top rotational EDGE option

key_additions.contract_summary: Traded (2028 6th for Kroeger + 2028 7th); existing rookie deal
key_additions.headline: Young punter acquired via minimal cost to replace departing Tommy Townsend

key_losses.headline: Best OL on the roster traded for a 5th-rounder — second straight year Houston dealt away its starting tackle

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded as part of Montgomery deal
key_losses.headline: 2023 2nd-round IOL depth piece traded to Detroit, deepening Houston's center concerns
2-time Pro Bowl RB cut after missing all of 2025 with foot injury; replaced by Montgomery
Slot depth receiver departs, thinning Houston's WR room behind Nico Collins
Young rotational LB signs with Atlanta, creating minor depth thinning at linebacker
35-year-old depth EDGE rusher not expected back; replaced by Logan Hall and Dominique Robinson
Veteran RB not expected to return; Montgomery trade directly replaces his role
